message:
feat(indexer): add forward-compatibility recovery for SQLite schema migrations

When a newer version of cass creates a database with a higher schema
version (e.g. v14+), the current binary would previously fail to open it
entirely.  This change introduces `open_storage_for_index()` which calls
`SqliteStorage::open_or_rebuild()` — if the schema version is from the
future, it backs up the existing DB file and recreates a fresh one so
indexing can proceed without data loss.

Key details:
- `open_storage_for_index()` returns a `(storage, rebuilt)` tuple so the
  caller can set `needs_rebuild = true` when recovery occurred, ensuring
  a full re-scan of all sources into the fresh database.
- On `MigrationError::RebuildRequired`, the original DB is preserved as
  `agent_search.db.backup.<timestamp>` alongside the new DB.
- A tracing::warn log records the recovery event with db_path, reason,
  and backup_path for post-mortem diagnosis.

Tests:
- Unit test `open_storage_for_index_recovers_from_newer_schema` seeds a
  DB with schema_version = CURRENT+1, verifies recovery succeeds with
  `rebuilt=true`, confirms the reopened DB has the current schema version,
  and asserts a backup artifact exists on disk.
- Integration test `index_handles_existing_schema_13_db` in cli_index.rs
  seeds a v13 DB and runs `cass index --json` end-to-end, verifying it
  succeeds without emitting "unsupported schema version 13" warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
